steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what both authors would agree on. Both Text 1 and Text 2 must directly support the correct answer — not just one text, and not something either text merely implies without stating.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Many new albums include more artwork than albums generally once included.": neither text argues that streaming services actively encourage longer albums — they describe a trend, not a platform directive. ✗
  • "Many new albums sell more copies than albums generally once sold.": neither text claims artists are padding albums with low-quality tracks. ✗
  • "Many new albums are longer than albums generally once were.": Text 1 states artists are "making albums that are much longer than albums from decades past"; Text 2 states "many new albums are longer and have more songs than albums crafted for physical formats." Both texts confirm this directly. ✓
  • "Many new albums are quieter than albums generally once were.": neither text claims listeners prefer shorter albums — the texts describe what artists are producing, not listener preference. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
